'''Ryan Lee Hudson''' (born 20 November 1979) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] ([[Reserve team|A-Team]]), [[England national rugby league team|England]] ([[Reserve team|A-Team]]), and [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]], and at club level for [[Stanley Rangers]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] (two spells), the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], the [[Castleford Tigers]] (two spells) ([[Captain (sports)|captain]]), and the [[Bradford Bulls]] (no appearances), as a {{rlp|so|hk|sr}}, or {{rlp|lf}}. He is also the brother of former ''[[Coronation Street]]'' and ''[[Wild at Heart (UK TV series)|Wild at Heart]]'' actress [[Lucy-Jo Hudson]].

Hudson was set to play for [[Bradford Bulls]] in 2005 but failed a substance test after taking some pain-killers abroad that contained the banned substance [[stanozolol]]. He was suspended from the game for two years; the ban expired shortly before the start of 2007's [[Super League XII]].

==Early career==
As a youngster, he played for amateur side [[Stanley Rangers]], whom he later went on to coach. Whilst at the club his potential was obvious. He picked up several player of the year awards and is regarded as one of the club's finest ever products.

==International honours==
In October 2001, Hudson was named as captain for England under-21s two-game tour against [[South Africa rugby league team|South Africa]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Hudson to captain U-21s|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1615151.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=3 January 2015|date=23 October 2001}}</ref>

Ryan Hudson represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] 'A' while at Castleford playing {{rlp|LF}} in the 12–34 defeat by New Zealand at [[Brentford F.C.]]'s stadium on 30 October 2002, playing {{rlp|LF}} and scoring one [[Try (rugby)|try]] in the 44–8 victory over Fiji at [[Lautoka]], Fiji on 9 November 2002, as a [[Rugby league positions#Substitutes|substitute]] in the 12–18 defeat by Fiji President's XIII at [[Suva]], Fiji on 13 November 2002, and {{rlp|LF}} in the 30–18 victory over Tonga at [[Nuku{{fakau'a}}alofa]], Tonga on 16 November 2002, and represented Great Britain 'A' playing {{rlp|HK}} and scoring one [[Try (rugby)|try]] in the 52–18 victory over New Zealand 'A' at [[Leeds Rhinos]]' stadium on 29 October 2003.<ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk">{{cite web|url=http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=25|title=Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk|publisher=thecastlefordtigers.co.uk |date=31 December 2013|accessdate=1 January 2014|url-status=unfit|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120415111900/http://www.thecastlefordtigers.co.uk/playerprofile.php?pid=25|archivedate=15 April 2012}}</ref>

==County honours==
Ryan Hudson won a [[cap (sport)|cap]] playing {{rlp|LF}} for [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] while at Castleford as the [[Captain (sports)|captain]] in the 56–6 victory over Lancashire at [[Odsal Stadium]], [[Bradford]] on 2 July 2003.<ref name="Statistics at thecastlefordtigers.co.uk"/>

==Post-playing career==
Outside of Rugby League, he has an endorsement deal with ''Maximuscle''. Hudson also co-owns a gym in [[Castleford]] with former [[Featherstone Rovers]] player [[Jamie Field]], he is also a coach at [[Stanley Rangers|Stanley Rangers ARLFC]].
